steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two complete thoughts: a main fact about fungi and trees, then an explanation of the mechanism. A sentence boundary is needed between them — the second thought opens with an introductory phrase that must attach to a full independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — no period between thoughts: fuses two independent clauses without adequate separation. ✗
  • Choice B — comma between thoughts: a comma alone can't join two independent clauses; creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — period after the first thought: correctly closes the first sentence; the second opens with its introductory phrase attached to a proper independent clause, and the parenthetical is set off cleanly by dashes. ✓
  • Choice D — transition phrase without a full clause: the second 'sentence' begins with a transitional phrase but lacks a complete independent clause, creating a fragment. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
